reasoning: In January 2025, the U.S. issued an Interim Final Rule establishing comprehensive AI export controls, including ECCN 4E091 for AI model weights, effective January 13, 2025. However, in May 2025, the new administration reportedly "rescinded and replaced" this rule, or "officially paused" the AI diffusion controls, opting instead for a policy statement/guidance focusing on end-use restrictions rather than formal new ECCN-based controls. A rescission or pause does not represent "new or expanded" export controls. With the knowledge cutoff at late October 2025, there has been no indication of a return to new binding regulations on cloud access or expansions of ECCN 4E091 this year. Given the administration's preference for guidance and end-use/end-user enforcement over structural AI tech controls, and with only two months left in 2025 to officially issue and bring into effect a new regulation, the probability of a "Yes" resolution is quite low. Therefore, a probability estimate of 25% is appropriate and consistent with the market trend.
